stories.title: DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel
stories.description: The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.

stories.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
stories.description: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.

stories.title: Iran hits oil tanker off Dubai as fighting continues on all fronts
stories.description: Iran attacked and set on fire a massive Kuwaiti oil tanker off Dubai overnight, as Gulf states increasingly suffer the fallout from the war.

stories.title: Iran's strike wounded over a dozen U.S. personnel and hit valuable jets in Saudi Arabia
stories.description: NPR has confirmed that at least two U.S. E-3 Sentry aircraft were damaged and more than a dozen U.S. service members were injured in an Iranian missile and drone attack in Saudi Arabia on Friday.

stories.title: How the US could try to seize Iran's Kharg Island
stories.description: Donald Trump has indicated he could send troops to take control of Iran's key oil export terminal.

stories.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
stories.description: The Israeli PM says Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church over concerns about Iranian strikes.

stories.title: Two Indonesian UN peacekeepers killed in explosion in Lebanon
stories.description: It is the second fatal incident in the last 24 hours, Unifil says, after Israel expanded its operation against Hezbollah.

stories.title: Potential Houthi threat to Red Sea shipping could further damage global economy
stories.description: The Iran-backed group could bring a second crucial waterway to a standstill, writes Sebastian Usher.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
stories.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib and Mohamed Ftouni, alleging they also belonged to Hezbollah's military wing.

stories.title: UN human rights chief calls on US to conclude probe into Iran school strike
stories.description: The strike - which killed at least 168 people, mostly children - "evoked a visceral horror", Volker Türk said.

stories.title: Ukraine signs deal with Saudi Arabia offering drone expertise
stories.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says the Gulf kingdom faces the same type of attacks from Iran that Kyiv has been resisting.

stories.title: Gaza peace doubts deepen as attention shifts to Iran
stories.description: With the world focused on the Iran war, there is increasing uncertainty about what happens next for Gaza.

stories.title: 'My daughter is under the rubble': Inside Tehran as civilian toll of strikes rises
stories.description: One month since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, Tehran residents tell the BBC their lives have been devastated.

stories.title: Is Trump's pause on attacking Iranian energy for diplomacy or an escalation?
stories.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

stories.title: How the Hormuz closure could affect food, medicines and smartphones
stories.description: The price of a wide range of goods - from food, to smartphones, to medicines - could be affected by the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Macron arrives in Japan on visit dominated by economic consequences of Iran war
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Tuesday arrived in Japan for a visit expected to be dominated by the economic fallout of the Middle East war. France and Japan have said they stand ready to take "all necessary measures" to ensure the stability of global energy markets.

stories.title: 'Dumb judges': Trump heckles Supreme Court ahead of pivotal birthright case
stories.description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, March 31: Donald Trump fumes at the Supreme Court as its judges prepare to hear arguments in a constitutional case over his 2025 executive order on birthright citizenship. Also, the Financial Times alleges that Pete Hegseth sought to make investments in de...

stories.title: As US, Israel and Iran continue fighting, question is how to end 'a war of attrition'
stories.description: US President Donald Trump threatened to destroy Iran's Kharg Island and power grid, were it not to agree to a deal the US sent to Iranian leaders to end the war. This was later followed by fresh strikes from the US and Israel, which Iran responded to by targeting Gulf nations. FR...

stories.title: Crude prices whipsaw on mixed signals from Iran war
stories.description: Oil prices searched for direction on Tuesday amid conflicting events related to the Iran war. While US President Donald Trump's threats that the US would obliterate Iranian energy infrastructure if the Strait of Hormuz remained closed led to an increase in prices, a later report ...

stories.title: Spain closes airspace to US planes involved in attacks on Iran
stories.description: Spain's airspace is now closed to U.S. planes involved in attacks on Iran, a step beyond its previous denial of use of jointly-operated military bases. Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez has become one of the most vocal critics of the US and Israeli attacks in Iran, describing them as ...

stories.title: Vital desalination plant in the Gulf become war targets
stories.description: US president Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iran's desalination plants if talks to end the war do not advance. Desalination plants across the Gulf region have become target during the one-month old war being vital civilian infrastructure targeted as vulnerabilities by Ira...

stories.title: Three UN peacekeepers in Lebanon killed in 24 hours
stories.description: In Lebanon, two Indonesian peacekeepers were killed in the south of the country on March 30 in an explosion. The UN agency tasked with policing the region said "an explosion of unknown origin destroyed their vehicle." It comes just a day after another Indonesian peacekeeper was k...

stories.title: Multiple explosions, airstrikes knock out power in Tehran
stories.description: Multiple explosions rocked Tehran on Tuesday, with news agencies reporting power outages in parts of the Iranian capital. Reporting from Tehran, FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayah says residents have been hearing “airstrikes, followed by the unnerving sound of thundering explosions," he says...

stories.title: Nadim Houry warns: Israel is no longer just fighting Hezbollah, 'it’s waging war on Lebanon'
stories.description: Jennie Shin is pleased to welcome Nadim Houry. According to Houry, the current conflict in Lebanon reflects not only an immediate military escalation but a deeper structural impasse shaped by decades of unresolved tensions. What we are witnessing is not simply a confrontation bet...

stories.title: Middle East war live: US defense chiefs give update on war in the Middle East
stories.description: US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Dan Caine were to give an update Tuesday on operations against Iran as the conflict continued to engulf the region. US launched strikes near one of Iran’s main nuclear sites while Tehran set ablaze a K...

stories.title: Italy denies the US military use of its air bases to attack Iran
stories.description: Italian press reports that the government has refuse to authorise US to land bombers at a base in Sicily

stories.title: ‘Iranians don’t consider Trump’s 15-point plan as beginning of diplomacy’
stories.description: ‘Iranians don’t consider Trump’s 15-point plan as beginning of a diplomatic track’

stories.title: Iran war wipes $120bn off Dubai, Abu Dhabi stock markets
stories.description: The UAE's financial markets have been some of the hardest hit by the fallout of the US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: Iran war: What is happening on day 32 of US-Israel attacks?
stories.description: In an exclusive interview with Al Jazeera, Secretary of State Rubio said US war objectives would be achieved in 'weeks'.

stories.title: Israel says four soldiers killed as army pushes deeper into south Lebanon
stories.description: Hezbollah attempts to make Lebanon ground invasion 'costly' for Israeli army as it continues its advance.

stories.title: Three scenarios for the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Two military scenarios and one diplomatic one emerge, with Pakistan playing a crucial mediatory role.

stories.title: South Korean president promises maximum response to energy crisis
stories.description: South Korean president Myung vowed to respond to the energy crisis caused by Iran war to the fullest extent possible.

stories.title: Drone attack sparks fire on Kuwaiti tanker in UAE amid Iran’s Gulf attacks
stories.description: Dubai authorities say blaze contained with no oil leakage or injuries reported, as attacks are reported across the Gulf.

stories.title: China to ‘strengthen’ cooperation with Pakistan on Iran as Dar visits Beijing
stories.description: China on Tuesday said it would strengthen cooperation with Pakistan on Iran as Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar arrived in Beijing on a brief visit for detailed discussions on the ongoing conflict. At the invitation of China’s FM Wang Yi, Dar is undertaking a ...

stories.title: Pakistan, seven other countries condemn Israeli curbs on Muslim and Christian worship in occupied Jerusalem
stories.description: Pakistan, along with seven other countries, condemned the restrictions imposed by Israel on the freedom of worship for Muslims and Christians in occupied Jerusalem, the Foreign Office (FO) said on Tuesday. Since Israel and the United States started a war with Iran on Feb 28, Isra...

stories.title: ‘Diplomacy takes time’: Pak envoy reaffirms Islamabad’s commitment to Iran-US peace amid complex situation
stories.description: Pakistan’s Ambassador to the US Rizwan Saeed Sheikh has reaffirmed Islamabad’s commitment to facilitating dialogue between the United States and Iran, even as regional tensions intensify. stories.title: Israel passes law making death penalty default sentence for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks
stories.description: Israel’s parliament approved a bill on Monday that would allow the execution of Palestinians convicted on terror charges for deadly attacks, a move that has been criticised as discriminatory and immediately drew a court challenge. Sixty-two lawmakers, including Prime Minister Ben...

stories.title: ‘All roads lead to higher prices and slower growth’: IMF says Iran war is dimming outlook for many economies
stories.description: The war in the Middle East has caused serious disruption to the economies of frontline countries, and is dimming the outlook for many economies that had just started to recover from previous crises, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) warned on Monday. In a blog published by th...

stories.title: Old world order coming apart, ceasefire in Iran: Lavrov’s statements
stories.description: Russia’s top diplomat stressed that Moscow calls for an immediate end to US and Israeli aggression against Iran, described the use of military force against civilians and civilian infrastructure as unacceptable

stories.title: Three thousand vessels blocked in Strait of Hormuz, transportation costs rise by 200%
stories.description: Transport companies are forced to restructure routes by combining sea segments with overland routes across the Arabian Peninsula

stories.title: Malaysian ships to be exempt from fee for sailing through Hormuz — minister
stories.description: Malaysia is a friendly country and maintains good diplomatic relations with the government of Iran, Anthony Loke Siew Fook said

stories.title: US intends to create global energy crisis with attacks on Iran — expert
stories.description: Dmitry Kuzyakin noted that earlier media reports indicated that the Pentagon had begun preparations for the operation dubbed "Final Blow"

stories.title: Over 200 Iranian people accused of cooperating with US, Israel
stories.description: On February 28, the United States and Israel launched a military operation against Iran, striking major cities, including Tehran

stories.title: IRGC deputy commander blames US for blocking global access to Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: General Ali Fadavi said that Washington miscalculated and set goals that were supposed to be achieved within three to five days, but it failed to achieve any of them

stories.title: US believes Iran’s new supreme leader is alive but seriously injured — Trump
stories.description: Nobody has heard from him, the US president said

stories.title: G7 countries powerless in Iran crisis — newspaper
stories.description: The publication notes that on Monday, the heads of finance and energy ministries and central banks of Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United States, and the United Kingdom held an "unprecedented joint meeting" to coordinate their response

stories.description: Any slightest movements by enemies will be suppressed with missiles and drones

stories.description: This strategic mistake by the American and Zionist enemies of Islamic Iran has humiliated them and set them on a path to destruction, Ibrahim Zolfaghari, representative of the Iranian Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, said

stories.title: Internet access down for over 740 hours in Iran — NetBlocks
stories.description: A chart shows that network connectivity remains at around 1% of normal levels

stories.description: According to Mohsen Paknejad, the necessary measures have been taken to ensure fuel security, and citizens can rest assured

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
